economic_notes: Council has passed a resolution to transfer $51,000,000 in investments to the Bijiinago, Noongom, Waabang Trust held separately from the First Nation; $1,000,000 transferred in 2023. Significant Williams Treaty and Islands in the Trent settlement funds held. Old Railroad Stop (gas bar and store) operated as enterprise. Ontario First Nations Limited Partnership distributions received.
notes: Fiscal year end March 31, 2023. Three consolidated subsidiaries: Zhooniya Makak GP Inc., Zhooniya Makak LP, AKI Land Holdings Inc. First year of adoption of PSA sections 3450 (Financial Instruments), 3041 (Portfolio Investments), 2601 (Foreign Currency), and 3280 (Asset Retirement Obligations) - resulted in $8,568,063 adjustment to accumulated remeasurement gains and $579,848 adjustment to net financial assets on adoption. Accumulated remeasurement gains of $3,656,125 reported separately. Large investments portfolio (~$96.9M) primarily comprised of Williams Treaty and Islands in the Trent settlement funds. Council committed to transferring $51M to Bijiinago, Noongom, Waabang Trust. Old Railroad Stop (gas bar/store) is a significant revenue source at $17.5M. OSR calculated as total revenue ($35,936,478) minus total government transfers ($13,319,583). Restricted cash includes Ontario First Nation Limited Partnership ($1,363,280), Lottery ($7,493), Williams Treaty settlement ($1,265,747), Islands in the Trent settlement ($3,041). No schedule of remuneration included in the extracted text.
